addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

/* Re-initialize a hash structure to its empty state.  The two head tables are
   fixed-size (65536 entries) while the per-window arrays (prev/hashval/same and
   their "2" variants) hold window_size entries.  All call sites pass
   ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE. */
void ZopfliResetHash(size_t window_size, ZopfliHash *h)
__CPROVER_requires(window_size == ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, window_size * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, window_size * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, window_size * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, window_size * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, window_size * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_assigns(h->val, h->val2)
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->same))
/* Current hash values are reset to zero. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == 0)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 == 0)

file.content:
#define HASH_MASK 32767

#define HASH_SHIFT 5

/*
Update the sliding hash value with the given byte. All calls to this function
must be made on consecutive input characters. Since the hash value exists out
of multiple input bytes, a few warmups with this function are needed initially.
*/
static void UpdateHashValue(ZopfliHash *h, unsigned char c)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
__CPROVER_assigns(h->val)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == ((((__CPROVER_old(h->val)) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
{
    h->val = (((h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^ (c)) & HASH_MASK;
}

/*
Prepopulates hash:
Fills in the initial values in the hash, before ZopfliUpdateHash can be used
correctly.
*/
void ZopfliWarmupHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
                      ZopfliHash *h)
{
    UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 0]);
    if (pos + 1 < end)
        UpdateHashValue(h, array[pos + 1]);
}

/*
Appends the length and distance to the LZ77 arrays of the ZopfliLZ77Store.
context must be a ZopfliLZ77Store*.
*/
void ZopfliStoreLitLenDist(unsigned short length, unsigned short dist,
                           size_t pos, ZopfliLZ77Store *store)
__CPROVER_requires(length < 259)
__CPROVER_requires(dist <= 32768)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(store, sizeof(*store)))
/* origsize == 3 is not a multiple of ZOPFLI_NUM_LL/ZOPFLI_NUM_D (so both
   cumulative-histogram fill loops are skipped) and not a power of two (so no
   ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA realloc occurs); every append simply writes index 3. */
__CPROVER_requires(store->size == 3)

content.text: I now understand the structure. Let me write the specification for `ZopfliUpdateHash`.

input.new_string:
void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
                      ZopfliHash *h)
/* The byte at index pos+ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH-1 is read (when in range), and the
   run-length scan reads array[pos] and array[pos+amount+1], all of which stay
   strictly below end; require the window of input up to end to be valid. */
__CPROVER_requires(end > 0)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end * sizeof(unsigned char)))
/* The hash structure: two hashes, each with head/prev/hashval arrays, plus the
   shared "same" run-length array. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
/* Head tables only ever hold -1 (empty) or a valid in-window index; this keeps
   the chain-head dereferences h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] in bounds. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    unsigned ik; ik < 65536 ==> (h->head[ik] >= -1 && h->head[ik] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
})
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    unsigned ik2; ik2 < 65536 ==> (h->head2[ik2] >= -1 && h->head2[ik2] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
})
__CPROVER_assigns(h->val, h->val2)
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->same))
/* Primary hash: val is rolled forward by exactly one UpdateHashValue step over
   the lookahead byte (or 0 when out of range), and stays in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == (((__CPROVER_old(h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^
    (pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0)) & HASH_MASK))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
/* The current position's hashval cell records the new hash, and the head table
   for that hash now points back at this position. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->hashval[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] == h->val)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->head[h->val] == (int)(pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK))
/* prev link for this slot stays inside the sliding window. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->prev[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
/* Secondary hash is derived from "same" at this slot xored with the primary
   hash, and the same head/hashval/prev invariants hold for it. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 == (((((int)h->same[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK]) - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH) & 255) ^ h->val))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 >= 0 && h->val2 <= HASH_MASK)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->hashval2[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] == h->val2)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->head2[h->val2] == (int)(pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->prev2[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
{
    unsigned short hpos = pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK;

void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
                      ZopfliHash *h)
/* The byte at index pos+ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH-1 is read (when in range), and the
   run-length scan reads array[pos] and array[pos+amount+1], all of which stay
   strictly below end; require the window of input up to end to be valid. */
__CPROVER_requires(end > 0)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(array, end * sizeof(unsigned char)))
/* The hash structure: two hashes, each with head/prev/hashval arrays, plus the
   shared "same" run-length array. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h, sizeof(*h)))
__CPROVER_requires(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->head2, 65536 * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->prev2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->hashval2, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(int)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(h->same, ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE * sizeof(unsigned short)))
/* Head tables only ever hold -1 (empty) or a valid in-window index; this keeps
   the chain-head dereferences h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] in bounds. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    unsigned ik; ik < 65536 ==> (h->head[ik] >= -1 && h->head[ik] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
})
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
    unsigned ik2; ik2 < 65536 ==> (h->head2[ik2] >= -1 && h->head2[ik2] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
})
__CPROVER_assigns(h->val, h->val2)
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->head2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->prev2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->hashval2))
__CPROVER_assigns(__CPROVER_object_whole(h->same))
/* Primary hash: val is rolled forward by exactly one UpdateHashValue step over
   the lookahead byte (or 0 when out of range), and stays in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val == (((__CPROVER_old(h->val) << HASH_SHIFT) ^
    (pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH <= end ? array[pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH - 1] : 0)) & HASH_MASK))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val >= 0 && h->val <= HASH_MASK)
/* The current position's hashval cell records the new hash, and the head table
   for that hash now points back at this position. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->hashval[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] == h->val)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->head[h->val] == (int)(pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK))
/* prev link for this slot stays inside the sliding window. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->prev[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
/* Secondary hash is derived from "same" at this slot xored with the primary
   hash, and the same head/hashval/prev invariants hold for it. */
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 == (((((int)h->same[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK]) - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH) & 255) ^ h->val))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->val2 >= 0 && h->val2 <= HASH_MASK)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->hashval2[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] == h->val2)
__CPROVER_ensures(h->head2[h->val2] == (int)(pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK))
__CPROVER_ensures(h->prev2[pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK] < ZOPFLI_WINDOW_SIZE)
{
    unsigned short hpos = pos & ZOPFLI_WINDOW_MASK;

lines: void ZopfliUpdateHash(const unsigned char *array, size_t pos, size_t end,
+/* The byte at index pos+ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH-1 is read (when in range), and the
+   run-length scan reads array[pos] and array[pos+amount+1], all of which stay
+   strictly below end; require the window of input up to end to be valid. */
+/* The hash structure: two hashes, each with head/prev/hashval arrays, plus the
+/* Head tables only ever hold -1 (empty) or a valid in-window index; this keeps
+   the chain-head dereferences h->hashval[h->head[h->val]] in bounds. */
+/* Primary hash: val is rolled forward by exactly one UpdateHashValue step over
+   the lookahead byte (or 0 when out of range), and stays in [0, HASH_MASK]. */
+/* The current position's hashval cell records the new hash, and the head table
+   for that hash now points back at this position. */
+/* prev link for this slot stays inside the sliding window. */
+/* Secondary hash is derived from "same" at this slot xored with the primary
+   hash, and the same head/hashval/prev invariants hold for it. */

content.text:
The result is **0/40 — verifies vacuously**, exactly matching the well-documented pattern for the multi-pointer hash/cache functions in this file (`ZopfliResetHash` 0/25, `ZopfliStoreLitLenDist` 0/31, `ZopfliFindLongestMatch` 0/157, `TryGetFromLongestMatchCache` 0/41).

`ZopfliUpdateHash` has **8 `is_fresh` objects** (`h` + 7 backing arrays + `array`) plus a run-length scan loop that can iterate up to 65535 times — far heavier than its already-vacuous siblings. The vacuity is confirmed by the fact that mutant 36 (`pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH` → `pos - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH`) survives even though it directly changes which byte feeds `UpdateHashValue` and would flip my exact `h->val == ...` postcondition — so the postconditions are never effectively reached under the `--depth` bound.

Per the established pattern (and the guidance not to keep trying when the kill score obviously can't be increased), I'll leave the strong, sound specification in place. Let me record this finding in memory.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-updatehash-vacuity
description: ZopfliUpdateHash verifies but 0/40; 8 is_fresh objects + up-to-65535-iter loop exceed depth bound → vacuous
metadata:
  type: project
---

`ZopfliUpdateHash` in zopfli.c verifies (exit 0) but mutation testing kills 0/40.

**Why:** Same depth-bound vacuity as the other multi-pointer hash/cache functions
([[cbmc-resethash-vacuity]], [[cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity]],
[[cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity]], [[cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity]],
[[cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity]]). The harness has 8 `is_fresh` objects
(`h` + head/prev/hashval/head2/prev2/hashval2/same + `array`) plus the
run-length scan `while` loop that can iterate up to 65535 times. Postconditions
are never reached under `--depth`, so even mutant 36 (`pos + ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH`
→ `pos - ZOPFLI_MIN_MATCH`, which flips the exact `h->val == ...` ensures) survives.

**How to apply:** Don't chase kills here. A strong sound spec is in place:
requires head/head2 entries are -1 or in-window (keeps `h->hashval[h->head[h->val]]`
safe), array fresh up to `end`, h->val in [0,HASH_MASK]; ensures the full
val/val2/head/head2/hashval/hashval2 update relations + prev<WINDOW bounds.
Leave it as-is.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliFindLongestMatch vacuity](cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity.md) — ~17 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/157; each __CPROVER_forall needs a unique quantifier var name
- [CBMC ZopfliResetHash vacuity](cbmc-resethash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/25; ~230K body loop iterations (2×65536 head + 3×32768 window clears) exceed depth bound so postconditions never reached; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliStoreLitLenDist vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/31; 8 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200, yet dropping any array is_fresh crashes goto-instrument; no non-vacuous middle ground

lines: - [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliFindLongestMatch vacuity](cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity.md) — ~17 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/157; each __CPROVER_forall needs a unique quantifier var name

- [CBMC ZopfliResetHash vacuity](cbmc-resethash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/25; ~230K body loop iterations (2×65536 head + 3×32768 window clears) exceed depth bound so postconditions never reached; strong sound spec left in place

- [CBMC ZopfliStoreLitLenDist vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/31; 8 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200, yet dropping any array is_fresh crashes goto-instrument; no non-vacuous middle ground

content.text:
Done. Summary of what I did for `ZopfliUpdateHash` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c`:

## Specification added
A strong, sound contract covering both the primary and secondary rolling hashes:

- **Preconditions** — `array` fresh up to `end` bytes (`end > 0`); `h` and all seven backing arrays (`head`/`prev`/`hashval` ×2 + `same`) fresh at their real sizes; `h->val ∈ [0, HASH_MASK]` (required by the `UpdateHashValue` callee contract); and a quantified invariant that every `head`/`head2` entry is `-1` or a valid in-window index — this is what keeps the chain-head dereferences `h->hashval[h->head[h->val]]` memory-safe under nondeterministic inputs.
- **Assigns** — `val`/`val2` plus whole-object writes to all seven arrays (indices are data-dependent on `h->val`).
- **Postconditions** — the exact rolled-forward value of `h->val` (one `UpdateHashValue` step over the lookahead byte, or 0 out of range), the derived `h->val2` formula, both bounded to `[0, HASH_MASK]`, the `hashval[hpos]==val` / `head[val]==hpos` updates for both hashes, and `prev`/`prev2` window bounds.

## Result
CBMC **verifies** (exit 0), but mutation testing kills **0/40** — it verifies *vacuously*. This is the same depth-bound vacuity already documented for every multi-pointer hash/cache function in this file. `ZopfliUpdateHash` carries 8 `is_fresh` objects plus a run-length loop that can run up to 65535 iterations, so postconditions are never reached under the `--depth` bound (confirmed: even mutant 36, which flips the exact `h->val` postcondition, survives).

Per the project guidance, I did not weaken or hack the spec to chase unreachable kills — the strong sound specification is left in place, and I recorded the finding in memory.
